body.purpose: Primary message content, settings descriptions, labels.

mono.purpose: Code blocks, tool arguments, terminal, diffs, file paths.

motion.name: duration-default
motion.purpose: Standard transition time for most interactions.

motion.name: blur-glass
motion.purpose: Backdrop blur strength for frosted panels.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Single column, full-width panels, stacked layout.

breakpoints.name: desktop
breakpoints.purpose: Multi-panel layout with sidebar, chat, and optional panels.

breakpoints.name: sidebar-collapsed
breakpoints.purpose: Icon-only sidebar mode before hover expansion.

components.name: Default Button
components.description: Ghost/default button for non-primary actions in toolbars, settings, and inline controls.
components.css: .ds-btn-default { padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 6px; border: none; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.04); color: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-default:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); } .ds-btn-default:active { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.09); }

components.name: Send Button
components.description: Circular send button in the chat input toolbar.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-send" aria-label="Send message"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="#212121" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="22" y1="2" x2="11" y2="13"/><polygon points="22 2 15 22 11 13 2 9 22 2"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-btn-send { width: 32px; height: 32px; padding: 0; border-radius: 50%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: all 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-btn-send:hover { background: #c0c0c0; transform: scale(1.05); } .ds-btn-send:active { transform: scale(0.95); } .ds-btn-send:disabled { opacity: 0.35; cursor: not-allowed; transform: none; }

components.name: Chat Input
components.description: Multi-line text input for composing user messages to the agent.
components.css: .ds-input-chat { width: 100%; padding: 0; background: transparent; border: none; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; outline: none; resize: none; max-height: 160px; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-input-chat::placeholder { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.40); } .ds-input-chat:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Tool Call Card
components.description: Collapsible card showing a tool execution — name, arg preview, status, and expandable detail pane.
components.html:
⚡bashnpm run build✓ successdetails
input
npm run build -- --production
output
Build completed successfully in 2.3s
components.css: .ds-tool-card { border-radius: 10px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); margin: 4px 0; overflow: hidden; } .ds-tool-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; cursor: pointer; color: #999; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; user-select: none; } .ds-tool-header:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); } .ds-tool-icon { font-size: 14px; } .ds-tool-name { font-weight: 600; color: #777; } .ds-tool-arg { color: #999; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; flex: 1; } .ds-tool-status.success { color: #34d399; margin-left: auto; } .ds-tool-status.error { color: #f87171; margin-left: auto; } .ds-tool-toggle { font-size: 11px; opacity: 0.5; } .ds-tool-detail { padding: 0 12px 8px; } .ds-tool-section-label { font-size: 10px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.40); margin: 6px 0 3px; } .ds-tool-code { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', 'Consolas', monospace; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.5; white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-all; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); background: rgba(0,0,0,0.2); padding: 8px; border-radius: 4px; margin: 0; }

components.name: User Message Bubble
components.description: User's chat message rendered as a right-aligned bubble with optional image thumbnails.
components.html: Can you refactor the auth middleware to use the new session store?
components.css: .ds-user-msg { display: flex; flex-direction: row-reverse; gap: 8px; width: 100%; margin: 4px 0; } .ds-user-bubble { max-width: 75%; padding: 10px 16px; border-radius: 10px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-word; }

components.name: Thinking Block
components.description: Collapsible accordion for the agent's internal reasoning trace.
components.html:
▸ Thinking…
Let me analyze the request step by step. First, I need to understand the session store pattern they're using…
components.css: .ds-thinking { margin: 4px 0; } .ds-thinking-summary { cursor: pointer; font-size: 12px; color: #888; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; user-select: none; padding: 4px 0; } .ds-thinking-summary:hover { color: #999; } .ds-thinking-content { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', 'Consolas', monospace; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.5; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.60); white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-word; margin: 4px 0; padding: 8px 12px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.02); border-radius: 6px; }

components.name: Settings Tab
components.description: Horizontal tab bar used in the settings slide-over panel.
components.html: <nav class="ds-tab-bar"><button class="ds-tab ds-tab-active">General</button><button class="ds-tab">Appearance</button><button class="ds-tab">Providers</button><button class="ds-tab">Agent</button></nav>
components.css: .ds-tab-bar { display: flex; gap: 0; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); padding: 0 16px; } .ds-tab { padding: 10px 16px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.60); background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; margin-bottom: -1px; } .ds-tab:hover { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); } .ds-tab-active { color: #c0c0c0; border-bottom-color: #a0a0a0; }

components.name: Session Sidebar Item
components.description: Individual session entry in the project sidebar.
components.html: Refactor auth middleware
components.css: .ds-session-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 4px 12px 4px 16px; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.60); transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-session-item:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); color: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); } .ds-session-item.active { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.88); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.09); } .ds-session-fav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 18px; height: 18px; padding: 0; background: none; border: none; color: inherit; cursor: pointer; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-session-item:hover .ds-session-fav { opacity: 0.35; } .ds-session-fav:hover { opacity: 1 !important; } .ds-session-fav.favorited { opacity: 1; color: #fbbf24; } .ds-session-name { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; flex: 1; }

narrative.overview: pi-kot's interface lives at the intersection of a finely tuned code editor and a modern chat surface. It's a workspace where a developer and an AI agent collaborate — not a generic chatbot wrapper. The aesthetic is dark-native with a glassy crispness: deep solid backgrounds, frosted floating panels, and an accent palette that signals state without demanding attention. Three visual dialects coexist: the chat surface (clean prose, markdown-rendered, streaming-aware), the tool execution timeline (collapsible cards with monospace detail panes), and the terminal panel (pure dark utility, xterm-native, no glass).
keyCharacteristics: Glassy but not decorative — every blur has a purpose
Smart disclosure as a design principle, not an implementation detail
Three visual dialects (chat / tools / terminal) under one token system
Subdued accent palette — grays and muted tones, not saturated primaries

rules.name: The Restraint Rule
rules.body: The accent color occupies ≤10% of any given surface. Its rarity makes it meaningful. Accent is for edges, focus rings, and interactive indicators — never for large background fills or decorative gradients.

rules.name: The Glass-Over-Solid Rule
rules.body: Floating panels use var(--bg-frosted) with backdrop-filter blur, never opaque overlays. The solid background (var(--bg-solid)) is always visible underneath, maintaining spatial hierarchy.

rules.name: The Only-Sans Rule
rules.body: No display font pairing. Inter at varying weights and sizes carries the full hierarchy. One family is right for a product UI; a second display face adds noise, not signal.

rules.name: The Mono-Confined Rule
rules.body: Monospace is bounded to code-shaped content: tool call IO, terminal streams, diff views, file paths, and inline code spans. Prose and labels never use the mono stack.

rules.name: The Zero-Shadow Rule
rules.body: No drop shadows on any surface. Depth comes from blur, transparency layering, and border contrast. A shadow implies weight; pi-kot's surfaces feel weightless.

dos: Use the glass-over-solid layering for panels and dialogs — the solid canvas must always be partially visible underneath.
Collapse tool call details by default. Smart disclosure keeps the chat surface readable.
Use mono for all code-shaped content: tool IO, terminal, diffs, file paths.
Tint every neutral toward the theme's brand hue. Even a barely-perceptible chroma (0.005–0.01) prevents gray from feeling dead.
Match accent usage to purpose: ≤10% of any given surface, reserved for interactive edges and focus indicators.
donts: Use box shadows anywhere. Depth comes from blur and transparency, not shadows.
Apply glass effects to the terminal panel. Terminal earns a distinct visual dialect — pure solid, no blur, no frills.
Use gradient text (background-clip: text) or decorative glassmorphism. Accent is a single solid color.
Use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Reuse the same card pattern across different contexts. Chat bubbles, tool calls, and settings cards each have distinct shapes and token assignments.
Use display fonts or decorative type anywhere in the UI.
Make the accent the primary action background at rest. Accent appears on hover/focus only.
